Statement: The only even prime number is 2.
Every composite number can be expressed as a product of prime numbers.
The HCF of co-prime numbers is 1.
The LCM of co-prime numbers is the product of the numbers.
A number divisible by 9 has sum of digits divisible by 9.
A number divisible by 3 has sum of digits divisible by 3.
If a number is divisible by 2 and 3
The number 825 is divisible by 3 and 5.
The smallest prime number greater than 50 is 53.
The smallest number divisible by 9 and 12 is 36.
The smallest number divisible by 12 and 15 is 60.
A number divisible by 10 must end with 5.
A number divisible by 5 ends with 0 or 5.
Two numbers whose product is 180 and HCF is 6 are 6 and 30.
The sum of digits divisible by 3 indicates divisibility by 3.
A number divisible by 4 has its last two digits divisible by 4.
A number divisible by 8 has its last three digits divisible by 8.
The HCF of two co-prime numbers is 2.
The LCM of two co-prime numbers is their sum.
The smallest composite number greater than 10 is 12.
